full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Selecting an appropriate toy dataset for testing arc-cosine kernels
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Help me choose an appropriate toy dataset from sklearn.datasets for testing arc-cosine kernels. The paper tests on grayscale image data (rectangles, convex shapes, MNIST variants). Provide code to load, preprocess (normalize to [0,1]), and split into train/val/test with stratified sampling and random seed 42.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Recommended sklearn digits dataset (8x8 handwritten digit images) as the closest match to the paper's image-based benchmarks. Provided MinMaxScaler normalization and stratified train/val/test split code.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Changed the test/val split ratios from the suggested 0.2/0.1 to 0.3/0.15 to get a larger test set for more reliable accuracy estimates. Added the visualization code for sample images myself.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified digits dataset has 1797 samples with 64 features. Confirmed MinMaxScaler maps to [0,1]. Checked stratification preserves class ratios across splits.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Write a 3-5 sentence justification for using the sklearn digits dataset to test arc-cosine kernels. Mention: what the dataset is, why it's a reasonable testbed for the paper's kernels, and what limitations it has vs the paper's MNIST variants.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Produced a clear justification connecting the dataset structure (flattened pixel vectors) to the paper's input format, while honestly acknowledging the smaller scale (64 vs 784 features, 1797 vs 12000+ samples).
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified feature counts and sample sizes for both our dataset and the paper's datasets. Confirmed that the paper's datasets are indeed more challenging due to background noise.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Help me choose a toy dataset from sklearn for testing arc-cosine kernels with preprocessing and splitting code.
top_5_prompts.why_important: Determined the dataset used throughout all of Question 2 and Question 3 experiments.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Write a 3-5 sentence justification for using sklearn digits to test arc-cosine kernels.
top_5_prompts.why_important: Ensured the mandatory dataset justification was honest about limitations while explaining the choice.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Task 2.1 of Part B.
